title: Bidens elliptica
taxonomy: Bidens elliptica is a species of plant commonly known as elliptic beggar-ticks or oval beggar-ticks. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, which includes other flowering plants such as daisies, sunflowers, and zinnias. The genus Bidens is a group of plants native to tropical and temperate regions, characterized by their small, daisy-like flowers and fibrous stems. Bidens elliptica is native to the southeastern United States, where it can be found growing in wetlands and along streams. This plant is known for its ability to form dense stands and can be considered a nuisance in some areas due to its tendency to spread quickly.
uses: Bidens elliptica, also known as the Bidens elliptica plant, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin conditions.
